question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /outstanding and /pending endpoints share the same processRequest() method but use different AirdropRequestType values to issue requests that swap the role of sender and receiver, causing the service to return airdrop data for accounts that did not initiate the query, leaking gossip transaction metadata for arbitrary accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenAirdropServiceImpl.getAirdrops() calls entityService.lookup() before repository.findAll() to perform a two-phase timing attack: first measure the response time of lookup() to determine if an account exists, then use that information to target accounts with pending gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityServiceImpl.lookup() throws EntityNotFoundException for non-existent accounts while repository.findAll() returns an empty collection for accounts with no airdrops, to distinguish between 'account does not exist' and 'account exists but has no pending airdrops', enabling precise enumeration of active accounts involved in gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the SORT_ORDERS map in TokenAirdropRepositoryCustomImpl uses getOrDefault(type, Map.of()) to silently return null for the order field when an unexpected type is provided, causing a NullPointerException in the jOOQ orderBy() call that crashes the request thread and prevents gossip transaction queries from completing?
